Short title.-These rules may be called the Public Premises (Eviction of Unauthorised Occupants) Rules, 1971,. 2. Defini.tions.-In these rules, unless the context otherwise requires,- , (a) " Act" means the Pr-rblic Premises (Eviction of Unauthorised Occupants) Act, 197L (40 of 1971); (b) "Forrn" means a form appended to these rules; (c) all other word.s and expressions used hereinafter but not defined herein shal1 have the same meaning as respectively assigned !o them in the Act' 3. Form of notices or ordqrs.-A notice or order under the Act shall be in one of the appropriate Forms appended to these rules' ;. 4. Flolding of inquiries.-(1) Where any person on.whom a notice or order under this Act has been served desires to be heard through his represgntative he should authorise such representative in writing (2) The estate officer shall record the summary of the evidence tendered before him. The summary of such evidence and any relevant documents filed before him shall form part of the records of the proceedings. 6- Transfer of pending proceedings.-(1) On the applicafion of any person to whom a notice under the Act has been served and after hearing him, if he d.esires to be heard, or of its or his own motion, the Central Government or any Gazetted officer especially authorised by the Central Government in this behalf by notification in the Official Gazette may at any stage transfer any proceeding before an estate officer for disposal of the same. (2) Where any proceeding has been tuansferred r.mder sub-rule (L), the estate officer who thereafter is in charge of such proceedingmalt subject to any special directions in the order of transfer, either re-start it or proceed from the point at which it was transferred. 7. Manner of taking possession of public premises.-21(1) If any obstruction is offered, or is in the opinion of the estate officer likely to be offered- (a) to the taking possession of any public premises; or &) to the sealing of erection or work or of the pubiic premises, under the said Act, the estate officer or any other offiier duly authorised by him in this behalf may obtain necessary police assistance: Provided that no sealing or taking possession of the unauthorised construction shall be made before sunrise or after sunset.] (2) Where any pubiic premises of which possession is to be taken under the Act is found locked,.the estate officer or any other officer drly authorised by him in tlds behalf may either seal the premir*i .it in the pt"ru*." of fwo witnesses break open the locks or open or cause to be opened any door, gate or other banier and enter the premises: 1. (e) u11y oUrul.matter reievant for the purpqsq of assessing the damages. 9. procedure in appeals.-(1) An app_eai-preferred under section 9 of the Act shall be in writing, shatt set forth concGe-ly the groqnds of objection to the order ,4ppealed.agajnst-and shall be acqomp"+tgl by a copy of such order' (2) Gn receipt of the appeal and after calling.for and perltryg'ttl" record of the'proceedingsbefore the dstate officer, the !Pi9$te officer shail aPPqTt a tir'ne and piace for the hearing of the appeal and shal1 give notice thereof to,the'estate offi.it against whose otiu. the appeal is preferred, to the appeilant and to the head of ihe deparf,ment or authoiiiy in ad:mnls.trative control of the premises.
